description: Provenance marker for candidates that are HAND-OWNED kernels or oracle targets, NOT points a generic search produced. Per docs/generic-low-level-search-goal-scope.md ('Relationship To Owned Kernels'): an owned kernel is a valid default route and a valid oracle target / structural guide, but is NOT evidence that the generic search space can express the same primitive. Tagging these prevents hand-owned wins from being mistaken for generic-search results.
members_examples: owned_flash_tile_gqa_whole (extra/qk_owned_flash_decode.hip) -- uses excluded v_dot2 + cross_lane_reduction; see decode_attention_gfx1100_v1
q4k_gemv_warp_kernel (extra/q4_k_gemv_primitive.py) -- uses excluded cross_lane_reduction; see decode_ffn_gemv_gfx1100_v1
excluded_primitives: n/a -- see the family manifest for the primitives the owned kernel relies on that the renderer cannot emit
classification_detail: These candidates ship as defaults because they pass W==D + correctness + route gates, but they are hand-written escape hatches. The generic-search milestone (owned-kernel lesson transferred) is reached only when the same primitive class becomes representable by search/codegen without hand-writing the whole kernel (Milestone 6).
